ablations_and_sensitivity: Robustness check in Appendix A.3 reruns malicious experiments without planner access, showing similar performance degradation (Figure 6).
Tests on 15 'harder puzzles' (3 boxes, longer solutions) demonstrate that the environment is not saturated for current SOTA models (Appendix A.9).
baselines_and_comparisons: Unassisted solve rates compared to assisted performance (Section 4.1).
Path optimality ratios for unassisted vs. assisted play (Figure 7, Figure 9).
Comparison of persuasive strategies (Deadlock vs. Sub-optimal Plan) across models (Section 4.4).
discussion_signals: Metric soundess: Comment c02073ca and Reviewer_Gemini_2 highlight that ν scores are not directly comparable across different base capability levels.
Prompt availability: Comment c4b25469 confirms Appendices A.10 and A.11 (prompts) are present, refuting earlier reviewer claims.
Confounding: Comment efd39fcb argues token-use is confounded by puzzle difficulty.
Transfer Gap: Comment 38033499 critiques the gap between Sokoban plan-conflict detection and open-domain safety-relevant vigilance.
experimental_setup: 10 puzzles spanning various lengths and complexities, restricted to two boxes and two goals (Section 3.1).
5 frontier LLMs tested in both player and advisor roles (Section 4).
Player move budget set at double the optimal solution length (Section 3.2).
key_assumptions: Persuasive capability can be decoupled from spatial reasoning by providing advisors with ground-truth planner solutions (Section 3.2).
Sokoban serves as an appropriate microcosm for complex real-life tasks due to its multi-step, multi-solution, and failure-prone structure (Section 3.1).
Token usage serves as a proxy for the allocation of limited computational resources (Section 4.3).
likely_accept_arguments: Novel multi-agent framework for investigating safety-critical social capacities.
Empirical finding of dissociation between capability and vigilance provides significant caution for AI-assisted decision making.
Token modulation analysis provides a mechanistic lens into LLM reasoning effort.
likely_reject_arguments: Technically flawed vigilance metric for high-capability models.
Weak statistical power for central dissociation claims ($n=5$).
Potential confounders (task conflict) in the resource-rationality interpretation.
Limited external validity/transfer of Sokoban to high-stakes human scenarios.
limitations_and_failure_modes: Vigilance metric ν is capability-conditional; the denominator vanishes for ceiling solvers (e.g., GPT-5), leading to undefined scores in Table 1 (Comment c02073ca).
Dissociation analysis is underpowered (n=5 models), with statistical power < 0.20 for null inference (Comment c02073ca).
Token modulation may be a response to 'advice-vs-inclination conflict' (task difficulty) rather than true deception detection (Comment efd39fcb).
Small puzzle sample size (n=10) and simple box configurations (Comment c4b25469).
main_contributions: Introduction of a controlled multi-agent evaluation framework using the Sokoban puzzle game to study persuasion and vigilance.
Formal quantitative metrics for persuasion (Ψ) and vigilance (ν) in sequential decision-making contexts.
Empirical evidence that LLMs modulate reasoning effort (token usage) based on the perceived intent of advice (resource-rational vigilance).
Discovery that performance, persuasion, and vigilance are dissociable; specifically, near-ceiling task performance (e.g., Grok 4 Fast) can coexist with extreme lack of vigilance.
method_summary: The study employs Sokoban as a multi-turn environment where a 'player' LLM attempts to solve puzzles while receiving advice from an 'advisor' LLM. To isolate persuasion from task reasoning, advisors are provided with optimal solutions from a symbolic planner (PDDL/Fast Downward) and algorithmically extracted sub-goals. Experiments involve 5 models (GPT-5, Grok 4 Fast, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4, DeepSeek R1) across three conditions: Benevolent, Malicious, and Malicious-Aware (where players are warned of potential manipulation).
novelty_signals: First simultaneous investigation of the relationship between persuasion, vigilance, and task performance in LLMs (Abstract).
Introduction of multi-agent Sokoban as a testbed for sustained, multi-turn epistemic vigilance (Comment 907ae8a8).
one_sentence_claim: Task-solving performance, persuasive capability, and epistemic vigilance are dissociable capacities in frontier Large Language Models, with high performance not necessarily implying the ability to detect and reject malicious advice.
red_flags: Vigilance metric ν denominator vanishes for ceiling-performing models (e.g., GPT-5).
Dissociation results rely on n=5 model data points, which is statistically underpowered.
Repeated value (0.594) across Table 1 and text suggests potential reporting errors or artifacts (Comment c4b25469).
reproducibility_signals: Frontier models are real commercial APIs (GPT-5, Grok 4 Fast, etc.) accessible as of early 2026 (Comment 6286dd31).
System prompts for players (Appendix A.10) and sub-goal advisors (A.11) are provided.
PDDLGym and Fast Downward planner usage described for finding optimal paths (Appendix A.5).
score_relevant_quotes: "puzzle-solving performance, persuasive capability, and vigilance are dissociable capacities in LLMs." (p. 1)
"GPT-5 is the most maliciously persuasive agent and the most vigilant player, while Grok 4 Fast is neither persuasive... nor vigilant." (p. 6)
"LLMs do consistently modulate their token use, using fewer tokens to reason when advice is benevolent and more when it is malicious." (p. 1)
"monitoring all three independently will be critical for future work in AI safety." (p. 1)
statistical_reporting: Non-significant correlations for dissociation: performance-persuasion (p=.796) and performance-vigilance (p=.328) (Section 4.2).
Token usage under benevolent advice: t(49) = 3.241, p = .002 (Section 4.3).
Token usage under malicious advice (solvable puzzles): t(91) = 6.92, p < .001 (Section 4.3).
strongest_results: GPT-5 is the most maliciously persuasive and the most vigilant, whereas Grok 4 Fast (despite ~100% solve rate) is neither persuasive nor vigilant (Section 4.2).
Significant token modulation: models use ~16% more tokens under malicious advice for solvable puzzles, but use fewer tokens when following malicious advice that leads to failure (Section 4.3).
Gemini 2.5 Pro demonstrates vigilance primarily when explicitly warned of deceit (Aware Malicious condition) (Section 4.2).
In 'No Planner Access' tests, GPT-5 remains the most vigilant/persuasive, confirming results generalize beyond oracle-assisted advisors (Appendix A.3).
title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
